bracketChallenge.format: Top two in each group plus eight best third-place teams; deterministic knockout scores.
scorerModelNotes: Squad-backed scorer pool regenerated from the official FIFA World Cup 2026 squad list PDF.
Weights combine current squad position, international goals/caps, and matched prior attacking-role weights when the prior player still appears in the official squad.
Played-match scorers in the tournament config override this pool.
sourceNotes: Matches 1-3 are locked as played results through the 2026-06-12T23:30:00Z data snapshot.
Round-of-32 slots use FIFA's official group-winner/runner-up slots and third-place slot eligibility from the FIFA World Cup 26 regulations.
Knockout scorelines are deterministic most-likely model scorelines; tied knockout scorelines are resolved in extra time for a bracket winner.

tiebreakNotes: Group standings sort by points, goal difference, goals for, conduct score, FIFA ranking-points prior, then seeded drawing lots.
Knockout penalty decisions blend match-model strength with historical shootout success where available.

sourceNotes: Groups checked against FIFA/search-visible standings and fixtures on 2026-06-10.
Group-stage fixture dates, kick-off times, venues, and cities are from FIFA's updated 2026 match schedule PDF and cross-checked against an accessible schedule table.

FIFA ranking prior uses FIFA FDCP men's ranking endpoint rows rechecked on 2026-06-12; FIFA's men ranking page was checked for the June 2026 official update cycle and records the next official update as 2026-07-20.
Official 48-team squad snapshot imported from FIFA SquadLists-English.pdf Version 1, published Friday 12 June 2026 21:07 UTC.
Played results are locked through the 2026-06-12T23:30:00Z snapshot: Mexico 2-0 South Africa, South Korea 2-1 Czechia, Canada 1-1 Bosnia and Herzegovina.
Venue weather inputs are historical June climate-normal style features, not matchday forecasts; use live forecasts when matches enter a reliable forecast window.
Injury, suspension, and recent-player-minutes fields are modeled as rating adjustments and currently default to zero unless source-noted in team adjustments.
Top two teams in each of 12 groups plus the eight best third-place teams advance to a 32-team knockout stage.
Bracket challenge output uses FIFA's Round-of-32 slots plus an Annex-C-style third-place slot eligibility resolver.
